title: Toward a unified benchmark and framework for deep learning-based prediction of nuclear magnetic resonance chemical shifts
methods: self-supervised pretraining (masked atom prediction, 3D coordinate recovery)

data_scope.pretraining_structures: >4.8 million 3D structures (Aflow + CSD + Materials Project combined)
data_scope.generalization_test: all nmrshiftdb2-2024 molecules with >=70 atoms (model trained on <=64 atoms)
data_scope.nmrshiftdb2_2024_split: 80:20 random split by InChIKey (no molecule overlap between train/test)

core_conclusion: NMRNet, a deep learning framework using the SE(3) Transformer with large-scale pretraining on >4.8 million crystal and molecular structures followed by fine-tuning on curated NMR datasets, achieves state-of-the-art chemical shift prediction for both liquid-state and solid-state NMR. The authors also introduce nmrshiftdb2-2024, the first systematically cleaned and expanded NMR benchmark, enabling unified evaluation across diverse chemical systems including large natural products (150+ atoms), solid-state materials, and stereochemistry determination tasks.
why_it_matters: Unifies liquid- and solid-state NMR prediction under one architecture; overcomes experimental data scarcity via large-scale pretraining; introduces a community benchmark (nmrshiftdb2-2024) as shared infrastructure; demonstrates practical utility in peak assignment (94% 13C accuracy), structure revision, and stereochemistry determination; ships as open code, data, and online service.
why_nature: Cross-disciplinary bridging of analytical chemistry, materials science, and machine learning; paradigm-shifting unification where prior frameworks were siloed by state; platform-type contribution (standardized benchmark + open online inference service); pretraining-then-fine-tuning methodology contribution for data-scarce spectroscopy problems.
added_value_to_my_research: Contrastive multi-modal pretraining strategy (structure + spectra alignment) maps onto multi-source urban data fusion (remote sensing + mobility + POI semantics)
Learned adaptive receptive field replacing fixed cutoff radius informs spatiotemporal KDE with locally learned bandwidths for urban health event mapping
Benchmark design pipeline (curate, deduplicate, split, document as FAIR artifact) provides a template for standardizing GeoAI evaluation datasets in health accessibility research
Log-weighted resampling for element-frequency imbalance addresses analogous spatial class imbalance in underserved peripheral areas in health geography ML models

Physics-constrained self-supervision (SE(3)-equivariance) supports the case for encoding spatial autocorrelation and flow-conservation priors into graph neural networks for accessibility and mobility prediction

figures.caption: Schematic of the NMRNet framework: data preparation (LSNMR and SSNMR inputs), self-supervised pretraining (masked atom prediction + 3D coordinate recovery), fine-tuning for chemical shift prediction, and frozen-model inference for downstream tasks.

Generalization of NMRNet: (a) prediction error vs. molecule size for 1H and 13C across in-distribution and out-of-distribution test sets; (b) two large natural product molecules (Yesanchinoside E, chiococcasaponin I); (c) predicted vs. experimental NMR spectra.
